scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The personal papers of Chuck Feeney.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Collection restricted to the permission of the curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Charles F. Feeney papers, #8698. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Chuck Feeney is a Cornell University alumnus, who graduated from the School of Hotel Administration in 1956 and was a brother of Alpha Sigma Phi and a member of the Sphinx Head Society. Making his fortune in duty free shopping, Feeney began his philanthropic efforts in 1982, with the founding of the Atlantic Philanthropies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Packet of faxed and emailed birthday greetings from various individuals including David Kennedy, Frank Mutch, Mike Windsor and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from Michael Bloomberg, Peter Beattie (Premier of Queensland, Australia, 1998-2007) and Bertie Ahern (Taoiseach of Ireland). Letter from Mariko Ando includes photographs. Card from Johanna Boeke contains photographs, including one of Feeney. Letter from Davis Coakley found enclosed a copy of Oscar Wilde: the Importance of Being Irish by Davis Coakley (box 18). Letter from Siobhan Commins found enclosed in a copy of A Secret History of the IRA by Ed Moloney (box 18).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from January 11, 2000 was found enclosed a copy of Prime Time: How Baby Boomers Will Revolutionize Retirememnt and Transform America by Marc Freedman (box 35).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Sushmita Ghosh and Bill Drayton of Ashoka found enclosed a copy of Leading Social Entrepreneurs Changing the World (box 34). Letters from Dr. David Hill found enclosed a copy of A Humanitarian Submission to Mr. Charles F. Feeney (box 18). Letter from R. Paul Herman found enclosed a copy of How to Change the World: Social Entrepreneurs and the Power of New Ideas by David Bornstein. Folder includes holiday cards from Bill Gates and Michael Higgins, President of Ireland. Letter from Le Ly Hayslip was found enclosed a copy of When Heaven and Earth Changed Places: a Vietnamese Woman's Journey from War to Peace (box 36). Note from Mark Patrick Hederman found enclosed a copy of The Haunted Inkwell (box 36).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder includes pictures of Feeney amd cards from Tom Mitchell. Letter from Betsy McBride found enclosed in a copy of It's a Terrible Day…Thanks Be To God (box 18). Letter from Colin McCrea includes newspaper clipping on Niall O'Dowd's autobiography and was found enclosed in An Irish Voice (box 19). Letter from Paul Murray found enclosed a copy of Agreement! The State, Conflict, and Change in Northern Ireland by Beatrix Campbell (box 18). Letter from Enda O'Coineen found enclosed a copy of the Unsinkable Entrepreneur by Enda O'Coineen (box 18). October 22, 1998 letter from Brendan O'Regan found enclosed in Live Youthfully Now by Russell A. Kemp (box 34).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Cornell President Hunter Rawlings. 1997 letter from T.A. Ryan was found enclosed in copy of 50 Treasures from the Hunt Collection, by Patrick F. Doran (box 18). Letter from Neal Ridge found in enclosed in The Japanese Mind: the Goliath Explained by Robert C. Christopher (box 34).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Card from Johannes Summatavet includes newsclippings, photographs of Feeney in Estonia in 1986. Letters from Ted Thoren include photographs, including ones of Chuck and Helga Feeney. Postcard from Connla (Stokes) found enclosed a copy of Dr. Pollock's Beard: More Recent Stories (box 35). Letter from F.A.O. Schwarz, Jr. (dated April 27, 1999) found enclosed a copy of Wait Till Next Year: a Memoir by Doris Kearns Goodwin (box 35). Letter from Robert H. Schuller found enclosed a copy of Prayer: My Soul's Adventure With God: a Spiritual Autobiography (box 35).

did.unittitle: Series II. Business and philanthropy files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alphabetical files on business and philanthropic matters. Included are several files on Cornell University, the Davney Fund, and GAGL (General Atlantic Group Limited).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Feeney kept a number of alphabetical subject files related to his business and philanthropic activities. It is likely that these files were kept at different locations, however there is little evidence to support definitive origins. While Feeney's various alphabetical files remain physically in original order, they have been intellectually integrated in order to better facilitate access by researchers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Binders kept by Feeney for Atlantic-related business trips taken to Ireland between 1993 and 2003. Chiefly represented are meeting agendas and background materials with John R. Healy, then the founding managing director of Tara Consultants Ltd (later Atlantic Philanthropies Ireland). Healy served as CEO for the Atlantic Philanthropies from 2001-2007. Also included are meeting materials for the Board of the University of Limerick Foundation, of which Feeney was a member.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: FACT was established in 1990 by Chuck Feeney and his then wife Danielle. Feeney's daughter Diane was the President of FACT from 1994 until 2013. She, Danielle, and her siblings served on the Board. FACT made its final grant in 2012.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files on the Giving Pledge. Included are materials for gatherings as well as correspondence between Bill Gates, Melinda French Gates, Warren Buffett, and Chuck Feeney.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Feeney arranged these files in roughly alphabetical order.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Founded in 2010 by Warren Buffett, Melinda French Gates, and Bill Gates, the Giving Pledge is a campaign encouraging billionaires to pledge a majority of their wealth towards philanthropy. Feeney officially pledged on February 3, 2011-- almost thirty years after establishing the Atlantic Philanthropies in order to give away his wealth. Other pledgers include Ted Turner, Diane von Furstenberg, and Michael R. Bloomberg.
